title: CNNs + Image Classification
subtitle: Pre-trained classifiers, ImageNet preprocessing, top-K predictions

exercises.title: Implement preprocess_for_imagenet
hints: Resize so the SHORTER side is 256 px (keep aspect ratio), then crop the center 224x224. This is what torchvision's `weights.transforms()` does — the canonical ImageNet pipeline.

Convert HWC uint8 [0,255] to CHW float32 [0,1], then subtract the ImageNet mean and divide by std. mean = [0.485, 0.456, 0.406], std = [0.229, 0.224, 0.225]. Add a batch dimension at axis 0 so the output is (1, 3, 224, 224).

exercises.title: Compute top-K accuracy
hints: argsort the logits row-by-row in descending order (numpy sorts ascending — sort `-logits` to flip). Slice the first `k` columns to get each row's top-K predictions.
A row counts as correct if the label appears anywhere in its top-K. Use broadcasting: `(top_k_indices == labels[:, None]).any(axis=1)`. Final accuracy is the mean of that boolean array.

exercises.title: Compare ResNet-18 vs MobileNet V3
self_checks: Ran the same image through both classifiers and compared the top-5
Found at least one image where the two models disagree on top-1
Wrote a 1-sentence reflection on when (or why) they disagree

self_checks_global: I understand the standard ImageNet preprocessing pipeline (resize-256 → center-crop-224 → mean/std normalize)
I can name the top-K predictions for a familiar image and roughly explain the model's mistakes
